title: blephariglottis lacera var. terrae-novae
intro: Blephariglottis lacera var. terrae-novae is an orchid species native to the island of Newfoundland in Canada. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as an emetic and purgative, which means it can induce vomiting and stimulate bowel movements. The health benefits of Blephariglottis lacera var. terrae-novae include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and indigestion, due to its purgative properties. Traditionally, the plant was used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which were administered orally to alleviate digestive discomfort and promote overall well-being.
